# Requirements Document Template

Copy this skeleton verbatim. All top-level sections are required — "None" or "N/A" is acceptable for sections that don't apply.

For multi-document projects (one doc per epic), use one `<topic>` per epic and one ID prefix per doc (e.g. `ORD-NNN`, `FUL-NNN`). Cross-doc dependencies are written as `Depends on: AUTH-003 (Requirements-auth-001.md)` — in-doc dependencies stay bare (`Depends on: ORD-002`).

```markdown
# [System Name] — Requirements Document

> Generated by `/devenv-gather-requirements`. See `session_memory-requirements.md` (if present) for in-progress session notes.

## 1. Vision

### 1.1 Purpose

[1–2 paragraphs: What this system is and why it exists. Enough context for a fresh reader with no prior knowledge.]

### 1.2 Actors

| Actor | Who they are | Goals | Experience expectations |
|---|---|---|---|
| [Name] | [Role, expertise, frequency of use] | [What they want to accomplish] | [How interactions should feel] |

### 1.3 Usage Scenarios

#### Scenario 1: [Actor] — [Goal]

[Narrative walkthrough, step by step. Include decision points, variations, and what happens when things go wrong. Stay at the user experience level.]

#### Scenario 2: [Actor] — [Goal]

[...]

### 1.4 Scope

#### In Scope

- [What the system does]
- [What the system does]

#### Out of Scope

- [What the system does NOT do] — *reason*

### 1.5 Constraints and Quality Expectations

- **Performance:** [e.g. Search results within 200ms at p95]
- **Security:** [e.g. All PII encrypted at rest and in transit]
- **Compliance:** [e.g. GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2]
- **Scale:** [e.g. 10,000 concurrent users, 1M records]
- **Integrations:** [External systems this must connect to]
- **Timeline:** [Hard deadlines if any]

### 1.6 Assumptions

- [Things taken as given that could change]

---

## 2. Requirements

### 2.1 [Functional Area — e.g. User Management]

#### REQ-001: [Title]

**Description:** [What the system does, from the user's perspective. No implementation details.]

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Given [context], when [action], then [outcome]
- [Additional criterion]

**Dependencies:** None

---

#### REQ-002: [Title]

**Description:** [...]

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [...]

**Dependencies:** [REQ-001](#req-001-title)

---

### 2.2 [Functional Area]

#### REQ-003: [Title]

**Description:** [...]

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [...]

**Dependencies:** [REQ-001](#req-001-title), [REQ-002](#req-002-title)

---

## 3. Priority Groups

> Stakeholder priority groupings — **not a delivery roadmap**. For component-level delivery sequencing with GitHub issues, run `/devenv-create-roadmap` after a blueprint exists. Each requirement here may later span multiple roadmap steps and multiple `Implementation_plan-*.md` files.

### Stakeholder Guidance

- **MVP rationale:** [Why this set is the minimum that delivers usable value]
- **Stakeholder priorities:** [Business reasons behind the ordering]
- **Open priority questions:** [Trade-offs the stakeholder hasn't decided yet]

---

### GROUP-01: MVP — [Name]

**Stakeholder rationale:** [One line: why these requirements come first from the business perspective]

**Priority:** MVP

**Requirements:**
- [REQ-001: Title](#req-001-title)
- [REQ-002: Title](#req-002-title)

---

### GROUP-02: [Name]

**Stakeholder rationale:** [One line]

**Priority:** P1 / P2 / Later

**Requirements:**
- [REQ-003: Title](#req-003-title)

---

### Reference Information

- [Link to GH issue, design doc, related PR, or external spec]

> **Companion episodes file:** If an `Episodes-<topic>-NNN.md` exists in this folder, it contains narrative illustrations of these requirements. Episodes are not normative — they don't override acceptance criteria.
```
